title: To require the Department of Defense to share best practices with, and offer training to, State and local first responders regarding how to most effectively aid victims who experience trauma-related injuries.
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Armed Services.
plain_summary: This bill tells the Department of Defense that it must share its best methods with local emergency workers like police officers, firefighters, and paramedics. The Defense Department has to teach these first responders how to help people who get hurt in scary or dangerous situations. The training will show local emergency workers the best ways to care for people who have been through traumatic events. This will help first responders in communities across the country do a better job helping trauma victims.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Armed Services.

sections.title: Section 1  Department of Defense Provision of Training and Information for First Responders Regarding Aid for Victims of Trauma-Related Injuries
sections.text: The Secretary of Defense must make sure that the Department of Defense shares its best methods and provides training to state and local first responders. This training will teach first responders how to best help people who have injuries caused by trauma. First responders are emergency workers like paramedics, firefighters, and police officers who arrive first at the scene of an emergency. Trauma-related injuries are serious physical or mental wounds that happen from violent events, accidents, or other harmful experiences.
